input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — standalone flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a code generation/guidance request, not a codebase modification task.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the breakdown:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — three `FlyoutItem` entries:

| Flyout entry | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single page, navigates directly |
| **Products** | Contains a `<Tab>` with two `ShellContent` children → renders as **Active** / **Archived** tab bar |
| **Settings** | Single page, standalone flyout entry |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a global route so you can navigate to it from either product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    { "Product", selectedProduct }
});
```

**Pages you still need to create** under a `Pages/` folder:
- `DashboardPage`
- `ActiveProductsPage`
- `ArchivedProductsPage`
- `SettingsPage`
- `ProductDetailsPage` (with `[QueryProperty]` attributes for parameters)

The key concept: pages declared in Shell's XAML tree get implicit routes. `ProductDetailsPage` isn't in the tree, so it needs `Routing.RegisterRoute` to be reachable via `GoToAsync`.
